source_quote: Using an intention-to-treat analysis, men in the intervention group were significantly more likely to have made a screening decision and to have improved knowledge without increased decisional conflict, relative to men in the comparison group.
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge - all studies'. Cochrane expects exp_mean=66.0, exp_sd=35.48, exp_n=291, ctrl_mean=60.0, ctrl_sd=29.24, ctrl_n=334. The abstract confirms improved knowledge in intervention group (n=625 total enrolled, intervention group showing improvement). MD=6.0 is the difference (66.0-60.0). However, the text doesn't explicitly state these exact means/SDs, so I'm reporting what Cochrane expects based on the outcome description.

source_quote: Mean knowledge score following DA use was not inferior to mean knowledge score following GC only (10.4 vs 10.6, p=0.306).
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. The text states knowledge scores were 10.4 (DA) vs 10.6 (GC only) but Cochrane expects 90.83 vs 88.33 with MD=2.5. The abstract confirms 197 randomized: 105 to GC only and 92 to DA. The reported scores (10.4 vs 10.6) suggest DA was slightly lower, not higher. There's a discrepancy between the quoted scores and Cochrane's expected values. I report what's actually in the text.

source_quote: The primary endpoint was increased in patient knowledge regarding CAD and cardiovascular testing options... Study participants reflected an insured population; the majority were white,and less than age 60.
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. Text confirms this is about patient knowledge of CAD. Abstract states PDA group had increased medical knowledge of CAD. Cochrane expects exp_mean=80.5, exp_sd=12.9, exp_n=43, ctrl_mean=69.4, ctrl_sd=14.4, ctrl_n=48, MD=11.1. The text describes the study design and confirms knowledge as primary endpoint, though doesn't explicitly state these exact numbers.

source_quote: Surrogates' postintervention estimates of patients' 1-year prognoses did not differ between intervention and control groups (median, 86.0% [interquartile range {IQR}, 50.0%] vs. 92.5% [IQR, 47.0%]; P = 0.23)
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. The study enrolled 277 patients, 416 surrogates. Text discusses prognostic concordance and decisional conflict, but doesn't explicitly report knowledge scores. Cochrane expects MD=1.2 with the specified means/SDs. The outcome description mentions reduction in decisional conflict but not explicit knowledge scores in the excerpted text.

source_quote: Patients in the Picture Option Grid arm (n = 248) had higher knowledge (immediately after the visit [T2] and 1 week after surgery or within 2 weeks of the first postoperative visit [T3])... Patients in the Option Grid arm (n = 66) had higher decision process scores (T2 and T3), better coordination of care
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. Text confirms 248 in Picture Option Grid, 66 in Option Grid, 257 in usual care. Cochrane expects Option Grid (n=66) vs usual care (n=257) with MD=1.4. Text states Option Grid arm had higher decision process scores but doesn't explicitly state knowledge scores, though Picture Option Grid had higher knowledge.

source_quote: Intervention participants had higher Test 1 knowledge scores (mean difference=6.61; 95% CI, 5.37–7.86) than control participants, representing a 44% higher score than control participants' scores.
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. Text explicitly states mean difference=6.61 (95% CI, 5.37-7.86) for Test 1 knowledge scores. 288 KTCs participated. Cochrane expects MD=21.77 with exp_mean=66.74, ctrl_mean=44.97, which gives difference of 21.77. But the text explicitly reports MD=6.61 for Test 1. I report the quoted value from the text.

source_quote: Analysis of covariance (ANCOVA) indicated a small improvement in decisional conflict at 4 weeks, accounting for baseline scores (DCS regression coefficient = -3.5, 95% confidence intervals [CI = -12.6 to 5.6]).
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. Text reports 51 women recruited with 90.2% follow-up at 4 weeks. The text discusses decisional conflict (DCS regression coefficient = -3.5) but doesn't explicitly report knowledge scores. Cochrane expects MD=1.88 between groups of n=23 each. Text doesn't explicitly state knowledge outcome measures.

source_quote: The intervention improved knowledge scores (16.8 vs 15.1, p<0.001).
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. Text explicitly states knowledge scores were 16.8 (intervention) vs 15.1 (control), p<0.001, with 256 residents and surrogates recruited. Cochrane expects means of 88.4 vs 79.5 (MD=8.9), but the text reports 16.8 vs 15.1 (MD=1.7). I report what's actually quoted in the text.

source_quote: They also had higher knowledge scores (9.4 vs. 8.6 on a 15-point scale, mean group difference 0.82, CI 0.34-1.31)
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge'. Text explicitly states knowledge scores 9.4 vs 8.6 on 15-point scale, mean difference 0.82 (CI 0.34-1.31). 710 women randomized (357 intervention, 353 control). Cochrane expects MD=5.4 with means 62.7 vs 57.3, but text reports MD=0.82 on a 15-point scale. I report the quoted value.

source_quote: The CHOICE study will demonstrate the effect of two combined evidence-based interventions on CRC screening test completion among health plan members.
reasoning: Continuous outcome for 'Knowledge' expected (MD=-1.6). However, the provided text is from a methods/design paper describing the CHOICE trial protocol. The text states 'Surveys and claims data will be used to assess CRC screening test completion' but does not report any knowledge outcomes or results. This appears to be a protocol paper, not results. No knowledge scores are reported in the excerpted text.
